hints: The repeated letters are L(3), O(2), P(2); only these can be both ends.
Fix two equal letters at the ends, then arrange the remaining 6.
solution:
LOLLIPOP has $L\times3, O\times2, P\times2, I\times1$. Only $L,O,P$ can occupy both ends. Fixing two equal letters at the ends and arranging the other six:
ŌĆó $L$: remaining $L,O,O,P,P,I\Rightarrow \tfrac{6!}{2!2!}=180$,
ŌĆó $O$: remaining $L,L,L,P,P,I\Rightarrow\tfrac{6!}{3!2!}=60$,
ŌĆó $P$: $60$.
Total $=180+60+60=300.$
The balance is $100(1.01)^{100}$. Using $\ln(1.01)\approx0.00995$, $(1.01)^{100}\approx e^{0.995}\approx2.705$, so the amount is about $\$270.$

hints: ╬Ė is opposite leg A, so tan ╬Ė = A/B; ╬Ė>ŽĆ/3 Ō¤║ A>ŌłÜ3┬ĘB.
P(A>ŌłÜ3 B)=Ōł½_0^{1/ŌłÜ3}(1ŌłÆŌłÜ3 b)db.
solution:
With legs $A,B$, the angle opposite $A$ has $\tan\theta=A/B$, so $\theta>\tfrac\pi3\iff A>\sqrt3\,B$. Then
$$P=\int_0^{1/\sqrt3}(1-\sqrt3\,b)\,db=\frac1{\sqrt3}-\frac{\sqrt3}{6}=\frac{1}{2\sqrt3}.$$
So $a=1,b=2,c=3$ and $abc=6.$

hints: Place the 4 odds first; they create 5 gaps for the 3 evens.
Choose 3 of the 5 gaps for evens: C(5,3); then arrange evens 3! and odds 4!.
solution: Arrange the $4$ odd numbers ($4!$ ways), creating $5$ gaps. Place the $3$ even numbers in distinct gaps so none are adjacent: choose $3$ of $5$ gaps ($\binom53=10$) and order them ($3!$). Total $=4!\cdot\binom53\cdot3!=24\cdot10\cdot6=1440.$

hints: Sums of symmetric dice are symmetric about their means (3.5m and 4.5n).
P(S<T)=P(S>T) iff SŌłÆT is symmetric about 0, i.e. means equal: 3.5m=4.5n ŌćÆ 7m=9n.
Smallest: m=9, n=7 ŌćÆ product 63.
solution: Each die's distribution is symmetric about its mean, so $S_m$ is symmetric about $3.5m$ and $T_n$ about $4.5n$; thus $S_m-T_n$ is symmetric about $3.5m-4.5n$. We get $P(S<T)=P(S>T)$ exactly when this center is $0$: $3.5m=4.5n\Rightarrow 7m=9n$. The smallest solution is $m=9,n=7$, so $m^*n^*=63.$

hints: P(max=4)=P(maxŌēż4)ŌłÆP(maxŌēż3).
P(maxŌēżk)=(k/6)┬▓ for two dice.
Diagonalize: eigenvalues solve ╬╗┬▓ŌłÆ6╬╗+5=0 ŌćÆ 5 and 1, eigenvectors [1,2] and [1,ŌłÆ2].
Write v=[3,2]=2┬Ę[1,2]+1┬Ę[1,ŌłÆ2]; apply A^10 (scales by 5^10 and 1).
solution:
$A$ has eigenvalues $5,1$ (from $\lambda^2-6\lambda+5=0$) with eigenvectors $[1,2]^T,[1,-2]^T$. Writing $v=[3,2]^T=2[1,2]^T+1[1,-2]^T$,
$$A^{10}v=2\cdot5^{10}[1,2]^T+1\cdot1^{10}[1,-2]^T=\begin{bmatrix}2\cdot5^{10}+1\\4\cdot5^{10}-2\end{bmatrix}.$$
So $a=2,b=5,p=10,c=1,d=4,g=2$ and $bp+a+c+d+g=50+2+1+4+2=59.$

hints: Let f_i be your win probability when the ball is at distance i; each turn: keep (1/3, ends) or move (1/3 each).
Solve: f_0 = 5/11.
solution:
Let $f_i$ be the probability you keep the ball given it's currently $i$ seats away (you are $0$). Each turn the holder keeps (prob $\tfrac13$, you win only if $i=0$) or moves either way. With $5$ seats, symmetry gives $f_1=f_4,\,f_2=f_3$:
$$f_0=\tfrac13+\tfrac23 f_1,\quad f_1=\tfrac13 f_0+\tfrac13 f_2,\quad f_2=\tfrac13 f_1+\tfrac13 f_2\Rightarrow f_2=\tfrac{f_1}{2}.$$
Then $f_1=\tfrac25 f_0$ and $f_0=\tfrac13+\tfrac23\cdot\tfrac25 f_0\Rightarrow f_0=\tfrac{5}{11}.$

hints: Guess the majority color; expected reward at a state (b,y) works out to max(b,y).
E(b,y)=max(b,y)+ (b/(b+y))E(bŌłÆ1,y)+(y/(b+y))E(b,yŌłÆ1).
solution:
Always guess the majority color. The expected reward at state $(b,y)$ is $(b+y)\cdot\frac{\max(b,y)}{b+y}=\max(b,y)$, so
$$E(b,y)=\max(b,y)+\tfrac{b}{b+y}E(b-1,y)+\tfrac{y}{b+y}E(b,y-1).$$
With $E(b,0)=\tfrac{b(b+1)}2$: $E(1,1)=2$, $E(2,1)=2+\tfrac23\cdot2+\tfrac13\cdot3=\tfrac{13}3$, and $E(2,2)=2+\tfrac12\cdot\tfrac{13}3+\tfrac12\cdot\tfrac{13}3=\tfrac{19}{3}.$

hints: Loaded die: P(face i)=i/21, so P(odd)=9/21=3/7, P(even)=4/7. Fair die is 1/2 each.
Odd sum = (fair odd, loaded even) or (fair even, loaded odd).
solution:
The loaded die has $P(i)=\tfrac{i}{21}$, so $P(\text{odd})=\tfrac{1+3+5}{21}=\tfrac37$ and $P(\text{even})=\tfrac47$; the fair die is $\tfrac12$ each. An odd sum needs exactly one odd:
$$P=\tfrac12\cdot\tfrac47+\tfrac12\cdot\tfrac37=\tfrac12.$$

hints: Use the ATM BlackŌĆōScholes approximation C Ōēł 0.3989┬ĘS┬ĘŽā┬ĘŌłÜT (BrennerŌĆōSubrahmanyam).
Solve Žā = C/(0.3989┬ĘS┬ĘŌłÜT) with T=0.25, ŌłÜT=0.5.
solution: For an ATM option, $C\approx\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}S\sigma\sqrt T\approx0.3989\,S\sigma\sqrt T$ (BrennerŌĆōSubrahmanyam). Solving, $\sigma=\dfrac{C}{0.3989\,S\sqrt T}=\dfrac{2.3}{0.3989\cdot12\cdot0.5}\approx0.961.$

hints: Method 2 correctly applies It├┤ to ln(M┬▓): d ln M┬▓ = 2Žā dW ŌłÆ Žā┬▓ dt.
Method 1 computes dM┬▓ correctly but then wrongly sets d ln M┬▓ = dM┬▓/M┬▓ (ignoring It├┤'s correction).
So Method 1 is wrong at its last (5th) line: a=1, l=5 ŌćÆ 100┬Ę1+5 = 105.
solution: Method 2 applies It├┤'s lemma to $\ln M^2$ correctly, giving $d\ln M^2=2\sigma\,dW-\sigma^2\,dt$. Method 1 is fine through $\frac{dM^2}{M^2}=2\sigma\,dW+\sigma^2\,dt$, but its final step equates $d\ln M^2$ with $\frac{dM^2}{M^2}$ ŌĆö invalid, since the log has its own It├┤ correction. So Method 1 is incorrect at its $5$th line: $a=1,\,l=5$, giving $100a+l=105.$
